name: Maurice Rocco
abstract: Maurice Rocco (born Maurice John Rockhold; June 26, 1915 – March 24, 1976) was an American pianist, singer, and composer known for playing boogie-woogie piano and his disdain for using a piano bench. He was a top nightclub and theater draw in the 1940s, and made several film appearances.

concerts.label: Festival of Dance and Music: African Dances and Modern Rhythms
Festival of Dance and Music: African Dances and Modern Rhythms
Palestine Pioneers Foundation All Star Show

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: I think I was about the second, part of the second group that went over there. The first group that went over was Maurice Rocco and Pearl Bailey... And we were all living in the same flat in '48.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: We had Louie Armstrong and his band, Mr. Bill Robinson, The Delta Rhythm Boys, The Peter Sisters, Maurice Rocco, and the Nicholas Kids.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: And on the same bill at the Zanzibar was Bill Robinson, the Delta Rhythm Boys, the Peters Sisters, Maurice Rocco, Pee Wee Marquette.

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Donegan
about_quotes.evidence: There was the Capital Lounge, the used to book Maurice Rocco, and he was a boogie-woogie pianist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dorothy Donegan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Williams
about_quotes.evidence: You know like Maurice Rocco, but Maurice Rocco used to dance. He was a tremendous dancer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995
